message:
test(fuzz): add KeyId::derive_from_public_key domain-tag fuzz target (resolves 4lhsv)

KeyId::derive_from_public_key (kid.rs:35-43) computes
BLAKE3(b'fcp.kid.v2' || pubkey)[..8] — the 8-byte KID used in COSE
headers for routing verification + decryption. A regression that dropped
the domain tag would let derive(p) collide with raw BLAKE3(p)[..8] from
any other unkeyed-BLAKE3 use, enabling cross-context KID collisions.

Existing fcp-crypto fuzz coverage (crypto_hpke_open, x25519_dh,
ed25519_verify, crypto_shamir_reconstruct) does NOT touch the KID
derivation surface.

Seven properties:
- determinism on derive_from_public_key
- bit-level injectivity over distinct pubkey bytes
- try_from_slice length gate (non-8-byte → InvalidKeyLength)
- try_from_slice + as_bytes identity round-trip
- from_hex/to_hex round-trip preserves bytes
- hex case insensitivity (lowercase ⇔ uppercase)
- stable 8-byte length

Once-gated regression anchors:
- (a) derive(b"") MUST NOT equal hardcoded BLAKE3(b"")[..8] =
  [0xaf, 0x13, 0x49, 0xb9, 0xf5, 0xf9, 0xa1, 0xa6] — the domain-tag-
  drop regression target. No blake3 dep needed in fuzz crate.
- (b) try_from_slice on lengths {0, 1, 7, 9, 16, 32} MUST trip
  InvalidKeyLength with correct expected=8/actual fields

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -649,5 +649,12 @@ test = false
 doc = false
 bench = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_keyid_derive"
+path = "fuzz_targets/keyid_derive.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false
+
 [workspace]
 members = ["."]
